title: Subtract decimals and whole numbers (hundredths)
description: More challenging subtraction problems with whole numbers, tenths, and hundredths like 15-7.45 or 12.19-7.68

hints: There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see two ways we can solve it.

###Place Value

We can think in terms of hundredths:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}9.19 - 7\\\\
&=9.19 - 7.00\\\\
&=919\text{ hundredths} - 700\text{ hundredths}\\\\
&=219\text{ hundredths}\\\\
&=2.19
\end{align}$

###Place value table

Let's line up the numbers by their decimal places. 

 Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
- | - | - | - | -
 $\blueE{9}$ | $.$ | $\blueE{1}$ | $\blueE9$
 $\maroonD7$ | $.$ | $\maroonD{0}$ | $\maroonD0$

Now, let's subtract each place value column.

| Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
- | - | - | - | -
 | $\blueE{9}$ | $.$ | $\blueE{1}$ | $\blueE{9}$
$-$ | $\maroonD7$ | $.$ | $\maroonD{0}$ | $\maroonD0$
| $\underline{~~~~}$|  | $\underline{~~~~}$| $\underline{~~~~}$
 | $2$ | $.$| $1$ | $9$
There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see two ways we can solve it.

###Place Value

We can think in terms of hundredths:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}1.64 - 1\\\\
&=1.64 - 1.00\\\\
&=164\text{ hundredths} - 100\text{ hundredths}\\\\
&=64\text{ hundredths}\\\\
&=0.64
\end{align}$

| $\underline{~~~~}$|  | $\underline{~~~~}$| $\underline{~~~~}$
 | $0$ | $.$| $6$ | $4$
